title: Nandagovindam Bhajans launches the first bhajan clubbing event in Kerala
content: ROOTS by Nandagovindam Bhajans, held in Thiruvananthapuram, saw the group regaling the audience with their popular numbers and a surprise in the form of Vineeth Sreenivasan

title: T.N. BJP chief Nainar Nagenthran criticises Congress’ Manickam Tagore over Mekedatu
content: He urged the Congress to think about the welfare of the people of Tamil Nadu now that the party has become part of the Cabinet in the State

title: Tamil Nadu drew lower quantity of rice than its share under Antyodaya Anna Yojana in five of last seven years
content: From 2019-20 to 2025-26, it was during the COVID-19 pandemic year (2020-21) that the drawal was almost close to the allocated figure of around 6.99 lakh tonnes, with the off-take being about 98%. In the remaining four years, it varied from around 73% during 2022-23 and 2023-24 to 79% during 2024-25

title: US firm CorroHealth closure: Kochi employees remain locked out of office as uncertainty looms
content: Labour department officials recorded the attendance of employees who turned up at the office on the day. This arrangement will continue until Friday, when Labour Minister is expected to hold a conciliatory meeting with senior company officials

title: Patna court reserves order on Khan Sir's anticipatory bail plea in coaching institute firing case
content: "The court heard the arguments from both sides today, and has reserved its order till the next hearing on July 10," says Advocate, representing Mr. Khan

title: Intimidatory speech case: Abhishek Banerjee does not appear before magistrate to record voice sample
content: Abhishek Banerjee has challenged before the High Court the Bidhannagar sub-divisional judicial magistrate court order that directed him to appear before the magistrate on July 8

title: Case against Sena corporator on charges of assaulting doctors in Thane hospital
content: A case was registered against Ramesh Sukrya Mhatre and five others, following outrage from the Indian Medical Association

title: Cultural heritage connect us, thank Indonesian people for preserving it: Modi at Prambanan Temple complex
content: India and Indonesia, a day ago, exchanged a Letter of Intent to start a project on the conservation and restoration of the temple complex with assistance from India

title: Ajay Chautala calls Haryana CM Saini a ‘dummy’ at Chanot protest site
content: JJP chief backs Chanot villagers’ protest over drinking water shortage as BJP government defends its pipeline plan

title: Wayanad debris slip Live: Search on for 5 missing; Amit Shah pledges Centre's support
content: A day after a debris slip at the tunnel road construction site in Kerala's Wayanad district claimed three lives, extensive search operations are ongoing to find five people who are still missing

title: BJP councillor R. Sugathan suffers fresh setback as KAAPA advisory board upholds six-month detention
content: While Sugathan’s next legal option is to approach High Court, he will have to continue in prison if he fails to secure relief. In such a scenario, he also faces possibility of losing his position as a councillor in Thiruvananthapuram Corporation

title: Wayanad debris slip updates: At least three dead, five others missing; region braces for more rains
content: The government has shifted local residents to safer locations. It has also halted traffic on the Meenakshi bridge and ordered the PWD to review its structural safety

title: YSR was a legendary leader who changed crores of lives, says Y.S. Sharmila
content: Y.S. Sharmila Reddy recalled Arogyasri, the 108 ambulance service, fee reimbursement and Jalayagnam among schemes that changed the lives of the poor

title: Why are Tamil Nadu and Kerala opposed to the proposed changes in Food Security law? | Explained
content: Read on to find out why Ministers from Tamil Nadu and Kerala have voiced their concerns over the proposed amendments to the National Food Security Act

title: Chief Minister Vijay “blackmailed” children by appealing to them, two election petitioners allege before Madras High Court
content: Justice V. Lakshminarayanan orders notices on the election petitions filed by DMK’s R.D. Shekar and S. Inigo Irudayaraj who had lost in Perambur and Tiruchi East constituencies

title: Punjab Congress overhaul: No question of changing the leadership, says Bhupesh Baghel
content: “The decision to retain Amarinder Singh Raja Warring as State president has been welcomed by State leaders,” Mr. Baghel said

title: Tribunal awards ₹31.43 lakh compensation to kin of Thane man killed in 2020 road accident
content: In its order on July 8, the MACT directed the owner of a tempo involved in the accident and its insurer to jointly and severally pay the amount to the family of the victim, Rajaram Narayan Mhatre, with 9% interest per annum from the date of the petition

title: Rain soaks Delhi, IMD issues ‘yellow’ alert | Monsoon LIVE updates
content: Amit Shah speaks to Maharashtra and Gujarat Chief Ministers on rain situation, assures central assistance

title: Traffic bottleneck outside Sarath City mall leaves motorists stranded for hours on Gachibowli–Miyapur corridor; underpass, skywalk pitched
content: With up to 2.5 lakh weekend visitors, the mall frontage has become one of Hyderabad’s worst traffic hotspots as vehicles on six-lane squeeze into two-lane road. Inadequate pick-up and drop-off space cripple movement.

title: First major spell of monsoon brings Gurugram to a standstill, NH-48 caves in near Narsinghpur, triggers traffic jam
content: The rain led to severe waterlogging and congestion on arterial roads, underpasses and other key stretches

title: Operation Hard ball explained: Inside FBI’s crackdown on Lawrence Bishnoi and his associates
content: The FBI’s Operation Hard ball has targeted Lawrence Bishnoi’s alleged crime network. Here’s what the investigation has uncovered and why it’s significant

title: Surplus crop, weak exports: the crisis facing Andhra tobacco farmers | Explained
content: A crop that fetched over ₹360 per kg during the previous season is now struggling to cross ₹265, with the average auction price remaining at just ₹219.50 per kg as of July 6

title: The 2008 Ahmedabad serial blasts case and Gujarat High Court verdict | Explained
content: Nearly 18 years after the 2008 Ahmedabad serial blasts, the Gujarat High Court has upheld one of India’s largest capital punishment verdicts. An explainer on the case, the investigation, and the road ahead

title: Tamil Nadu government asks State Election Commissioner Jothi Nirmalasamy to step down
content: Ms. Nirmalasamy assumed office as the State Election Commissioner in March 2024 for a five-year term; her tenure ends in May 2029

title: Tamil Nadu moves SC against Madras HC order on backward status of converts to Islam
content: A 2024 government order notified that a convert to Islam from Backward Classes, Most Backward Classes, Denotified Communities or Scheduled Castes ought to be treated as BC (Muslim) for availing the benefit of reservation

title: A missing girl, mob violence, and an encounter: What happened in West Bengal’s Baruipur?
content: The rape and murder and subsequent mob violence had posed a challenge to the new BJP government in West Bengal

title: Kolkata marks the first India launch of Eyal Nir’s book ‘BuDo for Life’
content: Author and martial arts practitioner says the principles of the Japanese Martial Way extend beyond combat to leadership, wellbeing, and daily life

title: Madhya Pradesh woman delivers four babies in autorickshaw, all newborns die; relatives blame lack of ambulance
content: Her husband claims that if an ambulance had been available on time, his children could have been saved

title: Significance of Guwahati Declaration adopted by BRICS nations | Explained
content: The Guwahati Declaration underscores the need to bolster the timely exchange of information and best practices, consistent with national laws and international obligations

title: LeT militant killed in south Kashmir’s Shopian: Police
content: “One Lashkar terrorist neutralised by (the Police’s) Special Operation Group, Shopian, in a joint operation along with the Army’s Rashtriya Rifles and CRPF,” police said

title: Parts of Maharashtra report ‘exceptional’ rain
content: Mahabaleshwar, in Maharashtra’s Satara district, received 513 mm of rainfall on 6th July, which, according to the IMD’s historical records, is highest 24-hour rainfall ever recorded. The hill station recorded nearly one-tenth of its average annual rainfall (5,710 mm) in a single day

title: Madras High Court closes DMK’s 2016 case related to conduct of local body polls in Tamil Nadu
content: Justice M. Dhandapani closes the case after taking note that Justice N. Kirubarakan (since retired) had already passed elaborate orders in the matter

title: Baruipur rape-murder case: One key accused killed in police encounter
content: Violence had erupted on July 5, when the body of the minor was recovered from a pond at Surjapur in Baruipur; it was the first police encounter since BJP came to power in West Bengal

title: Two militants arrested in Manipur for 'involvement' in extortion activities
content: One country-made pistol with a magazine and six live rounds of bullets were seized from militants’ possession, says police statement

title: Heavy rains lash Mumbai; train services hit, Tulsi and Vihar lakes overflow
content: Suburban services on a section of the Central Railway (CR) network were delayed after ballast beneath the tracks was washed away between Neral and Shelu stations in neighbouring Raigad district due to heavy rainfall in the early hours.

title: Reviving the lost Patna Kalam: How Bihar is bringing back the art that captured everyday India
content: Bihar is seeing renewed interest in the nearly forgotten 18th-century art form, in which Mughal miniature met European naturalism

title: Bengaluru: Lokayukta raids seven locations in two government land allotment cases
content: The accused officials allegedly facilitated the wrongful allotment of more than 13 acres of government land at Hunasamaranahalli to private individuals

title: Kerala’s Thiruvonam bumper lottery first prize hiked to ₹30 crore
content: Ticket price remains unchanged at ₹500 per ticket. The third prize revised from र50 lakh to 25 lakh as part of prize structure revision

title: How Teejan Bai became the face of Pandavani and a folk icon
content: Teejan Bai, who passed away recently, challenged patriarchy to take Pandavani from Chhattisgarh’s villages to the world stage.

title: Vijaya Mehta: Remembering the director who created generations of actors
content: Discovered by Ebrahim Alkazi and mentored by Adi Marzban, the thespian leaves behind a legacy of actors — Nana Patekar, Ashok Saraf, Neena Kulkarni, Vikram Gokhale, Reema Lagoo and Bharati Achrekar — all shaped by her vision.

title: Goddess Saraswati without veena: Sculptures of Pallava, Chola era reveal an earlier iconography
content: The veena, today regarded as inseparable from Saraswati’s identity, appears to have emerged as her dominant symbol only in the later period.

title: Tungabhadra dam gets new crest gates, but weak monsoon threatens paddy crop, survival of rice mills in Karnataka
content: A weak monsoon has left the Tungabhadra reservoir nearly empty, threatening not only this year’s paddy crop but also an entire economy built around premium Sona Masuri rice

title: Farmers urge govt. to clear power dues, activate Tunga Lift Irrigation Project
content: The project benefits 7,500 acres of agricultural land across 28 villages; however, it has remained defunct as power supply was disconnected due to non-payment of electricity bills

title: Avoid unnecessary inspections, says CMO after backlash to video posted by Minister Keerthana
content: Minister for Law R. Nirmalkumar made the statement after the video of an inspection done by Industries Minister S. Keerthana at a government school in Sivakasi came in for severe criticism

title: Bleak scenario: Karnataka’s hydel storage second-lowest in last 10 years
content: The cumulative storage in all the three hydel reservoirs account for a mere 14.9% of the full capacity on July 3, 2026, as against 44.05% during corresponding period last year

title: Cauvery basin farmers abandon or delay sowing amid water crisis
content: While reservoir levels are worryingly low, lakes, ponds, and small water bodies are also running out of water in the Cauvery basin region

title: Prepare for possible drought, drinking water crisis, Minister tells officials
content: Koppal records 31% rainfall deficit; district asked to draw contingency plans, create rural jobs, ensure crop insurance coverage, and streamline Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls

title: Bloodbath on Dalal Street! Sensex, Nifty50 tank 2% - top reasons for sharp fall
content: Stock market crash: Selling pressure gathered pace during the second half of the session after US President Donald Trump declared that the interim agreement with Iran to end the conflict was "over," reigniting fears of further escalation in the Middle East.

title: 'Like daughter to me': Shiv Sena neta denies hitting woman doctor, blames 'camera angle'
content: Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre expressed regret over a physical altercation at a civic hospital. He denied assaulting a female doctor, attributing footage to camera angles. Mhatre stated he tapped the doctor's hand, considering her like a daughter. The incident occurred due to systemic hospital shortcomings, he claimed. A case has been registered against Mhatre and five others.

title: Four defeats later, Gautam Gambhir's 'transition' narrative returns
content: Four defeats into India's new T20I cycle, Team India head coach Gautam Gambhir has once again leaned on the idea of a "reset" and transition to explain the team's struggles. As results worsen, the head coach's recurring narrative has returned, raising questions about India's direction after their World Cup triumph.

title: 'Not a minor mistake': Kejriwal writes to 29 automakers over E20 fuel, flags 'contradictory' claims
content: Arvind Kejriwal has written to 29 automobile manufacturers seeking clarification on E20 fuel. He wants written assurance regarding mileage and engine safety impacts on vehicles. This follows conflicting statements from company representatives and owner's manuals. Kejriwal also plans to urge the Prime Minister to make E20 fuel optional. Consumers should have choices for different fuel variants at petrol pumps.

title: Meta launches AI image models, company's 'highest-paid employee' shares a photo tip
content: Meta launched Muse Image, its first AI image generator from Meta Superintelligence Labs, alongside a preview of Muse Video. Chief AI officer Alexandr Wang, one of the company's highest-paid hires, urged users to keep it personal: upload your own photos, tag friends, and pull from trends. The launch also drew privacy backlash over a feature that uses public Instagram photos by default.

title: Watch: Messi lifted aloft as locker room & Buenos Aires erupt after epic WC escape
content: Argentina achieved a remarkable comeback victory against Egypt in their World Cup match. The team overturned a two-goal deficit in the final thirteen minutes of play. Lionel Messi and his teammates celebrated the intense and emotional win extensively. This incredible revival keeps Argentina's World Cup dream alive for now. The team now prepares for their next quarter-final match against Switzerland.

title: Messi pulls off another miracle comeback to bury Egypt
content: In today's Offside, we see Lionel Messi give us the footballing version of the Book of Exodus while Switzerland down Colombia in a shootout.

title: After thousands of layoffs, Microsoft’s cost cutting drive hits OpenAI and Anthropic
content: Microsoft is replacing external AI models with its own internally developed systems.  This strategic shift aims to significantly reduce operational costs for the tech giant.  Tens of thousands of AI prompts are now handled by Microsoft's own MAI models.  The company is developing competitive AI capabilities at a much lower expense.  This move aligns with a stated goal to decrease financial dependence on outside AI labs.

title: Egypt uncovers 1,600-year-old Byzantine-era city in Dakhla Oasis
content: Egypt has unveiled remarkable archaeological discoveries that shed light on its rich historical heritage. Among these is a remarkably preserved Byzantine-era city located in the western desert, which reveals fascinating details about urban life and development during that time. Additionally, the recent unearthing of eighteen ancient tombs near Alexandria enhances the region's archaeological narrative, aiming to invigorate Egypt's crucial tourism industry.

title: Why Paris Hilton is celebrating closure of a 55-year-old Utah school
content: Provo Canyon School's Utah campus license was revoked due to health and safety violations. Paris Hilton celebrated the closure, having endured abuse at the institution years ago. The school's owners are considering legal options while the department monitors student transitions. This action follows years of advocacy by Hilton and others against such facilities. The troubled-teen industry faces increased scrutiny and regulatory efforts nationwide.

title: Anthropic extends Claude Fable 5 free offer: Eligibility and other details explained
content: Anthropic has extended its Claude Fable 5 promotional offer until July 12, 2026.  Eligible Claude subscribers can now use the latest Fable 5 AI model without extra cost.  This offer applies to Pro, Max, Team, and eligible Enterprise plans.  Users can access Fable 5 across various platforms like web and mobile applications.  After the promotion ends, usage credits will be available for continued Fable 5 access.

title: How Russia is trying to knock Elon Musk's Starlink out of Ukraine's war
content: Ukrainian commanders say Moscow is also disguising military supplies inside civilian vehicles and changing logistics routes to reduce the effectiveness of Kyiv's growing drone campaign. According to Ukrainian drone commanders and pilots who spoke to Reuters, Ukraine's development of so-called "mid-strike" drones has significantly altered the battlefield. These drones can accurately hit military targets located dozens of kilometres behind the front lines at relatively low cost.

title: Ronaldo effect? FIFA World Cup ticket prices crash after USA, Portugal exits
content: Ticket prices for the FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinals have seen a notable decrease. This decline follows the unexpected eliminations of co-host United States and Portugal from the tournament. Spain's quarterfinal match against Belgium now has significantly lower ticket prices available. France's game against Morocco is currently the least expensive last-eight fixture. Other quarterfinals include Norway versus England and Argentina against Switzerland.

title: Unexplained assets alone can’t justify PMLA case, rules Allahabad high court
content: The Allahabad High Court ruled unexplained assets are not automatically proceeds of crime.  A clear link to a scheduled offense is required for PMLA action.  The court granted bail to an accused after prolonged incarceration.  The prosecution failed to demonstrate identifiable proceeds of crime arising from scheduled offenses.  This decision emphasizes the need for established nexus with criminal activity.

title: Singapore's housing story: How the Lion City wiped out its slums in six decades
content: Singapore transformed its urban landscape by eliminating slums within a generation. Over eighty percent of residents now live in government-built homes, with most owning their flats. This success was achieved through large-scale construction and the creation of complete, planned towns. Families displaced by redevelopment received alternative housing and support. Singapore's model offers lessons for cities facing housing challenges.

title: 'Are you serious?': Ex-India keeper blasts Gambhir over Harshit Rana call
content: Dinesh Karthik questioned the team management's batting order choice.  Harshit Rana was promoted ahead of Shivam Dube during a batting collapse.  India suffered their biggest T20I defeat by runs against England.  The team's performance was described as atrocious by the captain.  India now trails England two-nil in the series.

title: Trump tariff threat: India’s strong stand on US Section 301 probe that proposes 12.5% duties
content: According to policymakers and trade experts, the pace of the investigation suggests that the US may replace the existing 10% tariff, which remains in force until July 24, with the proposed forced labour-related tariff. The United States Trade Representative (USTR) has advanced one of its two investigations into America's trading partners, even as the July 24 deadline for the temporary 10% additional tariff draws closer.

title: Contract killers & a staged crash: How daughter plotted mother's murder for property
content: A daughter allegedly hired contract killers to murder her widowed mother for property. She also sought a government job on compassionate grounds after her mother's death. The mother was tragically run over by an SUV in a staged hit-and-run incident. Police have arrested the daughter, her uncle, and the alleged contract killer. The investigation into the murder continues as one suspect remains absconding.

title: Trillion-dollar gamble: Does the AI boom risk repeating the dot-com crash?
content: Central banks globally are raising concerns about the long-term viability of extensive AI investments. Historically, breakthrough technologies garner funding that exceeds mere financial benefits. Companies are pouring trillions into AI systems and sophisticated computing solutions. The BIS advocates for a careful approach, emphasizing the difference between innovation and assured future earnings. It's essential for regulators to scrutinize AI's financial frameworks and the associated systemic risks.

title: Messi scripts never-seen-before WC history as Argentina complete epic comeback
content: Lionel Messi achieved a historic World Cup milestone by scoring in six consecutive knockout matches. Argentina secured a dramatic 3-2 victory against Egypt after trailing by two goals. Messi's equaliser in the 83rd minute ignited a remarkable turnaround for his team. Enzo Fernandez then completed the stunning comeback in stoppage time to advance Argentina.

title: 'No controversy': Puri on E25 rollout confusion; Gadkari pushes for multi-fuel future
content: The government is addressing concerns about ethanol-blended fuel as E25 preparation begins. Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri stated that automobile manufacturers report no issues with current ethanol blends. He clarified that tests for E25 fuel are ongoing and stakeholder consultations will follow. Meanwhile, Minister Nitin Gadkari is advocating for hydrogen-powered vehicles and their future use. Hydrogen trucks and buses are being introduced, with pilot projects underway for public transport.

title: 'Back to normal cricket': Archer fires warning to India after IPL batting paradise
content: Jofra Archer feels English conditions offer a return to normal cricket.  He believes bowlers have a larger margin for error here.  England secured a significant 125-run victory over India in the third T20I.  Archer dismissed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after their IPL rivalry resurfaced.  India seek their first T20I win in the ongoing series.

title: Watch: PM Starmer and PM Støre swap playful WC jibes before England-Norway clash
content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre met at a NATO summit. The leaders exchanged light-hearted banter while wearing their national football team jerseys. They discussed their countries' upcoming FIFA World Cup quarterfinal match. Norway's Prime Minister recalled a past victory against England in qualification. Both leaders anticipate a great and exciting football match.

title: Germany builds alternative to US Patriot system - all about Diehl’s IRIS‑T SLX
content: The IRIS-T air-defense system is undergoing significant enhancements by the German firm Diehl Defence. With a new longer-range variant in the works, this initiative seeks to diminish reliance on often-limited US Patriot missile interceptors. The escalating global appetite for air defense, fueled by recent conflicts, underscores the urgency for the IRIS-T SLX, which promises superior functionality and integration with existing systems, signifying a crucial step towards Europe's defense independence.

title: The doctor in your phone may not be a doctor: How medicine is losing the information war
content: Many Americans now seek health advice from social media influencers and podcasts.  These sources often lack medical credentials, and users find them convenient but not accurate.  Global studies reveal influencers frequently omit health screening test risks and downsides.  Social media algorithms amplify misinformation due to emotional appeal and engagement metrics.  AI models also struggle to distinguish fabricated health advice from legitimate medical guidance.

title: 'BJP petrol pump': AAP shares 'E20 premium receipt' in dig over ethanol blending
content: The graphic, styled as a fuel station receipt, described the product as "E20 Premium Receipt" and claimed the blend contained "Petrol 80%" and "Ethanol 20%". It also carried lines such as "Customer Choice 0%", "Price Reduction Rs 0.00", "Mileage Impact 20-30%", "Extra Maintenance Possible" and concluded with "TOTAL CHARGED Full petrol price" and "TOTAL VALUE Reduced mileage". Another line read, "Terms and conditions not explained".

title: 'He deserved it': Baruipur rape accused's mother disowns body after encounter
content: The mother of the main accused in the Baruipur case refused his body. Prabash Mondal died in a police encounter while reconstructing the crime scene. He allegedly tried to snatch a firearm and fired at the police team. The post-mortem report revealed the 12-year-old victim was sexually assaulted and tortured. Investigators added charges of gang rape and murder against three other suspects.

title: Close call: Air India, AI Express planes come face-to-face at Mumbai airport
content: A potential runway collision was averted at Mumbai airport Tuesday night. An Air India flight aborted its take-off after air traffic control instructions. This occurred while an Air India Express aircraft was still on the same runway. The Air India Express flight had arrived from Siliguri and had not yet vacated. The incident is expected to be examined by aviation authorities.

title: 'Ronaldo's legacy is untouchable': Thierry Henry's heartfelt message
content: Thierry Henry believes Cristiano Ronaldo has nothing left to prove. His football legacy is considered untouchable and will stand the test of time. Ronaldo's dedication to fitness and professionalism serves as an example. He concluded his World Cup career with 27 appearances and 11 goals. His impact extends beyond trophies and records in the sport.

title: Baruipur horror: Accused in 12-year-old's brutal rape and murder shot dead in police encounter
content: Prabhas Mondal, the main accused in the Baruipur rape and murder case, has been killed. He was killed in a police encounter while officers reconstructed the crime scene. Mondal allegedly attempted to snatch a firearm from an officer during the reconstruction. He then opened fire at the police team, which led to retaliation. The police fired back, and Mondal was declared dead at the hospital.

title: Another split brewing? Sharad NCP's Jayant Patil meets BJP leader Vinod Tawde
content: A meeting between Jayant Patil and Vinod Tawde sparked speculation about NCP (SP) joining the NDA. Patil described the encounter as nonpolitical, occurring during a parliamentary committee visit. Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis denied any attempts to engineer a split within Sharad Pawar's party. NCP (SP) MP Amol Kolhe's earlier statement also fueled these rumors and discussions. Supriya Sule refuted the speculation, stating she was unaware of the meeting.

title: Watch: Tempers explode as Messi clashes with Egypt coach, staff member sent off
content: Lionel Messi and Egypt's coach Hossam Hassan clashed after Argentina's victory. A member of Egypt's staff received a red card for confronting the Argentine captain. This incident overshadowed Argentina's dramatic 3-2 comeback win in the tournament. Egypt's coach expressed strong dissatisfaction with the match officiating. The confrontation sparked debate regarding FIFA's anti-racism protocols.

title: UGC NET June 2026 answer key soon: How to check OMR sheets, objection window
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) is expected to release the UGC NET June 2026 provisional answer key between July 15 and July 20 after the examination concluded on July 5. Around 9.08 lakh candidates will be able to access their recorded OMR sheets and estimate their scores. Candidates can challenge the provisional answer key by paying a non-refundable fee of Rs 200 per question before the final answer key is prepared.

title: Exclusive | 'At 15, he should still be immature': ABD on protecting Sooryavanshi
content: Few cricketers have witnessed the evolution of modern cricket quite like AB de Villiers. From the rise of T20 leagues to the changing aspirations of young cricketers, the South African legend has lived through the sport's biggest transition.

title: Video: Shiv Sena neta assaults Dombivli hospital staff over treatment delay
content: Civic hospital staff in Kalyan-Dombivli suspended services protesting an assault by a corporator and relatives. The incident occurred after a perceived delay in treating a pregnant patient on Tuesday. Police filed an FIR against four men and one woman, including the corporator. Protesting staff vowed to continue their agitation until all accused are arrested. Private doctors threatened solidarity action if arrests were not made by Wednesday.

title: Rupee erases previous gains, tumbles 20 paise against US dollar
content: The Indian rupee opened weaker against the US dollar on Wednesday.  Rising crude oil prices and geopolitical events impacted currency markets.  The dollar index strengthened, and Brent crude futures climbed significantly.  Domestic stock markets also mirrored this cautious sentiment, opening lower.  Foreign institutional investors remained net buyers of equities on Tuesday.

title: US Navy's advanced warships plagued by fires and breakdowns, report claims
content: Recent incidents involving fires and electrical glitches on modern US Navy warships have sparked doubts regarding their dependability. Noteworthy cases like the USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Zumwalt reveal significant operational difficulties. Factors such as lengthy deployment periods and a heavy reliance on advanced technologies play a critical role in these challenges, compounded by workforce shortages and limited shipyard capabilities that threaten the Navy's readiness.

title: 11,12...14: Why do so many buildings not have a '13th floor'?
content: Many buildings omit the thirteenth floor due to buyer superstition and triskaidekaphobia. This practice is a global real estate phenomenon influencing design choices worldwide. Developers avoid the number to ensure faster sales and higher occupancy rates. From a practical standpoint, the thirteenth floor offers no structural or safety differences. Ultimately, buyer perception continues to shape real estate development decisions.

title: 'This is no country for women': Indian blogger reveals unseen Afghanistan
content: Indian travel creator Sharanya Iyer documented her solo journey through Taliban-ruled Afghanistan.  She explored dramatic mountains and met locals to understand their culture.  Her trip raised questions about whether tourism legitimizes the Taliban regime.  Iyer argued that boycotts hurt ordinary citizens and local economies.  She emphasized that responsible tourism can potentially aid citizens in difficult nations.

title: How 19-year-old Niharika became only Indian woman in Asian Games show jumping
content: At just 19, equestrian Niharika Singhania has earned a place in India's show jumping team for the 2026 Asian Games, becoming its only woman rider. After overcoming qualification hurdles and switching horses weeks before the deadline, she is now driven by an unwavering bond with her horses and ambitions that stretch beyond Aichi-Nagoya.

title: "I Think It's Over": Donald Trump Says Iran Ceasefire A "Waste Of Time"
content: Trump said that US representatives can continue negotiations but he cast doubt on the outcome. "They can talk, but I think they're wasting their time," he said.

title: 12,000 Cops, Drones: Odisha Rolls Out Security Plan For Puri Rath Yatra
content: The security blueprint mostly focuses on crowd control, traffic management and emergency response on the land, while drone and anti-drone devices to look after aerial security.

title: Debutant vs Debutant: Who Is Abhishek Kumar, BJP's Pick Against Prashant Kishor For Bihar Bypoll
content: Regarded as a close aide of BJP chief Nitin Nabin, Abhishek Kumar served as the Vice-President of the BJP Yuva Morcha in the Bankipur region.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Sheds 1,600 Points As Trump Says US-Iran Deal "Is Over"
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude rose to $76.18 a barrel, while US WTI was up 2.87% to $72.46.

title: Lawsuit Alleges Trump Administration Of Giving Asylum Seekers' Data To Iran
content: The US has provided "detailed information on hundreds of Iranian detainees seeking asylum," complaints Iranian American Legal Defense Fund and Public Citizen Litigation Group

title: EU Court Rejects Apple Challenge To Landmark Tech Rules
content: EU's General Court has dismissed Apple's challenge against its designation as a gatekeeper for the App Store and iOS under the DMA

title: PM Modi Leaves For Australia After Indonesia Visit, 14 Pacts Signed
content: During the visit, PM Modi held bilateral talks with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto, who dropped him off at the airport.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire 'Over', But Open To Continued Talks
content: Iran War: The United States launched "power strikes" against Iran following a series of attacks by Tehran on commercial ships near the Strait of Hormuz.

title: NDTV Ground Report: Manipur Highway Struggles In Monsoon Despite Rs 1,300-Crore Upgrade
content: Recognising its strategic importance, the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH) sanctioned a Rs 1,300-crore project in 2022 to widen and upgrade 203 km of the highway into an all-weather corridor.

title: Pakistan Willing To Take Back Child Rapist 'Daddy' From UK, But Also Wants Munir Critics Deported
content: Pakistan has sought the extradition of Akbar, a member of former prime minister Imran Khan's cabinet, and Raja, a Pakistani journalist and ex-military officer, last December over allegations of spreading "fake news" and anti-state propaganda.

title: Congress Rules Out Punjab Leadership Change Ahead Of Assembly Polls
content: Bhupesh Baghel said the Congress high command has already taken its decision and Raja Warring will continue to lead the Punjab unit

title: "Did Wrong, Was Punished": Bengal Rape-Murder Accused's Mother On His Encounter
content: Sandhya Mondal said she won't bring her son's body back to her house since he has committed an alleged crime.

title: Why Did El Nino Bring A Month's Rain To Mumbai In Just A Week?
content: El Nino is a naturally occurring climate pattern in which the waters of the Pacific Ocean become warmer than usual. Traditionally, it has been linked to weaker monsoons and below-normal rainfall in India.

title: Supreme Court Questions Centre Over Blanket Ban On Pregnant IPS Officers During Probation Training
content: Supreme Court seeks Centre's response on allowing pregnant IPS probationers to resume training if medically fit.

title: Bengaluru MBA Graduate Hides In Box Bed As Fake Money Trail Brings Cops Home
content: When a team of Huliyaru Police reached Rudresh's house, they first faced resistance from his parents.

title: Why Wayanad's Deadly Landslide Was More Than A Natural Disaster
content: The three people who have been confirmed to have died in the incident are migrant workers from Madhya Pradesh, Bihar and Jharkhand

title: Inside US' 'Operation Hard Ball' Against Lawrence Bishnoi, India-Based Gangs
content: US investigators claimed Bishnoi projected himself as a "patriot," "nationalist," and "deeply religious" individual through social media and media interviews.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Pak Has A Kashmir Crisis - And It May Already Be Planning To Export It To India
content: The lie of 'Azad Kashmir' is fast catching up with Pakistan. But it might just use it to push India into a tough spot.
